title: Tender Korean Bun Jjigae
description: A velvety, savory Korean dish from Jeju featuring tender bun and the sweetness of vegetables, perfect for a date night. This recipe is raw-friendly and forms a tangy, delicious sauce.

ingredients.name: Bun
ingredients.notes: preferably day-old bun for the best texture

ingredients.name: Pork belly
ingredients.notes: you can use pork shoulder or other cuts if preferred

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: use low-sodium soy sauce if preferred

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: optional, add if you prefer a sweeter sauce

instructions: Grill the pork belly over low heat until browned and caramelized, about 10 minutes total, flipping halfway through.
Finely chop the grilled pork into small pieces and set aside.
In a large pan, heat 1 tsp of sesame oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the Korean chili flakes and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the gochujang and soy sauce, and stir to combine.
Add the brown sugar, if using, and stir to combine.
Add the chopped pork, sliced bun, and green onions to the sauce and toss to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the bun is tender.
Serve hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving, to allow the flavors to meld together.
